package.name: forzen-core
package.description: Core game files for the forzen retro engine

quickStarts.name: GameObject
quickStarts.summary: Primary scene entity in the runtime. `GameObject` is the engine's equivalent of a Unity game object: it owns a transform, optional physics/collision state, arbitrary components, lifecycle hooks, and parent/child hierarchy relationships. Game-specific behavior is usually added by attaching Component instances.

quickStarts.name: Component
quickStarts.summary: Reusable behavior module that can be attached to a GameObject. Components are the main authoring surface for game logic in the engine. A component receives the same lifecycle hooks as its owning object, and can read or mutate the owner through gameObject after it has been attached.

quickStarts.name: GameDriver
quickStarts.summary: Browser frame driver for a Game. `GameDriver` connects the game loop to `requestAnimationFrame`, handles fixed timestep catch-up, renders through CanvasRenderer, forwards input state to the global Input singleton, and coordinates audio pausing when the browser backgrounds the page.

quickStarts.name: Assets
quickStarts.summary: Global asset manager used by engine helpers and generated game code. Game code usually imports this object directly: ```ts const name = Assets.getString(R.string.player_name); const sprite = Assets.getBitmap(R.drawable.player_idle); ``` Before bootstrap installs a real manager, this is a small dummy provider that throws for most asset types and returns the fallback for localized strings.

quickStarts.name: Language
quickStarts.summary: Global language bridge for game code. `Language` gives gameplay systems a single way to detect the player's current language across web, editor preview, Android, iOS, and desktop shells. Native hosts can install a LanguageProvider before startup; game code can then call `Language.getLanguage()` without caring where the value came from.

quickStarts.name: Prefab
quickStarts.summary: Runtime prefab registry. A prefab is a named factory that creates one or more configured GameObject instances. Providers self-register through PrefabProvider, and gameplay code can spawn them by id.

quickStarts.name: Vector
quickStarts.summary: Static vector math helpers for common gameplay calculations.

entries.name: Bitmap
entries.summary: Indexed pixel-art bitmap. `Bitmap.data` is a row-major string where each character is a palette glyph and `_` represents transparency. A bitmap can carry an optional ColorFilter; renderers use it to map glyphs to RGB colors.

details.text: Transformation helpers return new `Bitmap` instances. They do not mutate the source bitmap except for setFilter, which intentionally updates the filter in place for fluent asset setup.

fast paths. rotatedegrees(degrees: number): bitmap rotateradians rotates the bitmap by radians using nearest-neighbor sampling. right-angle rotations use exact fast paths. rotateradians(radians: number): bitmap scale uniformly scales the bitmap using nearest-neighbor sampling. scale(scalar: number): bitmap scalexy scales the bitmap on each axis using nearest-neighbor sampling. scalexy(scalex: number, scaley: number): bitmap setfilter assigns a color filter to this bitmap and returns the same bitmap. setfilter(filter: colorfilter): bitmap data row-major palette glyph data. property data: entries.name: Camera
entries.summary: 2D camera viewport used by world rendering and culling. `x` and `y` describe the top-left logical world position when scale is `1`. When scaled, visible-world bounds are computed around the viewport center so zooming keeps the center stable.

entries.name: CameraComponent
entries.summary: Component that makes its owner drive the global camera. Attach this to a player or camera target object. During `lateUpdate`, the component centers CAMERA on the owner so all world draw calls become relative to that target.

parameters.name: args
parameters.summary: Component arguments. Supports `scale`.

members.summary: Attaches this component to a game object. Component subclasses can override this to initialize engine state that must exist as soon as the component is added, such as colliders or rigidbodies.

entries.name: CanvasRenderer
entries.summary: High-level renderer facade for the engine's software pixel buffer. `CanvasRenderer` exposes drawing methods in two coordinate spaces: world-space methods apply the global CAMERA, while methods containing `Static` draw directly in screen/UI coordinates. The game loop calls beginWorldDraw and endWorldDraw around world drawing so zoomed camera rendering can scale sprites and primitives consistently.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.summary: Logical render size and default palette.

parameters.name: canvas
parameters.summary: Destination HTML canvas.

entries.searchText: canvasrenderer class graphics/canvasrenderer.ts high-level renderer facade for the engine's software pixel buffer. `canvasrenderer` exposes drawing methods in two coordinate spaces: world-space methods apply the global camera, while methods containing `static` draw directly in screen/ui coordinates. the game loop calls beginworlddraw and endworlddraw around world drawing so zoomed camera rendering can scale sprites and primitives consistently. constructor  canvasrenderer(properties: graphicsproperties, canvas: htmlcanvaselement): canvasrenderer beginworlddraw enables camera-scale-aware world drawing until endworlddraw. beginworlddraw(): void clear clears every pixel to transparent/empty. clear(): void drawbitmap draws a bitmap with its top-left corner at a world-space point. drawbitmap(x: number, y: number, bitmap: bitmap, filter: colorfilter | null): void drawbitmapcentered draws a bitmap centered on a world-space point. drawbitmapcentered(x: number, y: number, bitmap: bitmap, filter: colorfilter | null): void drawlightbitmap queues a bitmap-shaped light in world-space coordinates. drawlightbitmap(x: number, y: number, intensity: number, bitmap: bitmap): void drawlightbitmapcentered queues a centered bitmap-shaped light in world-space coordinates. drawlightbitmapcentered(x: number, y: number, intensity: number, bitmap: bitmap): void drawlightline queues a line-shaped light in world-space coordinates. drawlightline(x1: number, y1: number, x2: number, y2: number, intensity: number, thickness: number): void drawlighttext queues bitmap-font text into the light buffer in world-space coordinates. drawlighttext(text: string, x: number, y: number, font: font, kerning: number, intensity: number): void drawline draws a line in world-space coordinates. drawline(x1: number, y1: number, x2: number, y2: number, color: rgbcolor, thickness: number): void drawstaticbitmap draws a bitmap in screen-space coordinates. drawstaticbitmap(x: number, y: number, bitmap: bitmap, filter: colorfilter | null): void drawstaticbitmapcentered draws a bitmap centered on a screen-space point. drawstaticbitmapcentered(x: number, y: number, bitmap: bitmap, filter: colorfilter | null): void drawstaticbitmapscaled draws an integer-scaled bitmap in screen-space coordinates. drawstaticbitmapscaled(x: number, y: number, scale: number, bitmap: bitmap, filter: colorfilter | null): void drawstaticbitmapscaledcentered draws an integer-scaled bitmap centered on a screen-space point. drawstaticbitmapscaledcentered(x: number, y: number, scale: number, bitmap: bitmap, filter: colorfilter | null): void drawstaticlightbitmap queues a bitmap-shaped light in screen-space coordinates. drawstaticlightbitmap(x: number, y: number, intensity: number, bitmap: bitmap): void drawstaticlightbitmapcentered queues a centered bitmap-shaped light in screen-space coordinates. drawstaticlightbitmapcentered(x: number, y: number, intensity: number, bitmap: bitmap): void drawstaticlightline queues a line-shaped light in screen-space coordinates. drawstaticlightline(x1: number, y1: number, x2: number, y2: number, intensity: number, thickness: number): void drawstaticlighttext queues bitmap-font text into the light buffer in screen-space coordinates. drawstaticlighttext(text: string, x: number, y: number, font: font, kerning: number, intensity: number): void drawstaticline draws a line in screen-space coordinates. drawstaticline(x1: number, y1: number, x2: number, y2: number, color: rgbcolor, thickness: number): void drawstatictext draws bitmap-font text in screen-space coordinates. drawstatictext(text: string, x: number, y: number, font: font, kerning: number, filter: colorfilter | null): void drawstatictextstroked draws screen-space bitmap-font text with an outline stroke. drawstatictextstroked(text: string, x: number, y: number, font: font, strokecolor: rgbcolor, kerning: number, filter: colorfilter | null, strokewidth: number, minimal: boolean): void drawstatictextwrapping draws wrapped bitmap-font text in screen-space coordinates. drawstatictextwrapping(text: string, x: number, y: number, maxwidth: number, font: font, kerning: number, linespacing: number): void drawtext draws bitmap-font text in world-space coordinates. drawtext(text: string, x: number, y: number, font: font, entries.name: Collision
entries.summary: Static collision query helpers. These methods perform read-only overlap checks. They do not apply impulses, move objects, or invoke collision callbacks. For simulation, use the normal `Game` physics step or `GameObject.move`.

members.name: checkIfCollidersCollide
members.summary: Collider-based collision check. Accepts base Collider and dispatches by type tag. Callers should ensure colliders are synced first if owner transforms changed.

signatures.name: checkIfCollidersCollide
signatures.summary: Collider-based collision check. Accepts base Collider and dispatches by type tag. Callers should ensure colliders are synced first if owner transforms changed.

members.name: checkIfObjectsCollide
members.summary: Collider-based object collision check. Objects with `ignoresCollision` are treated as non-colliding. Use checkIfObjectsOverlap to ignore that flag.

signatures.name: checkIfObjectsCollide
signatures.summary: Collider-based object collision check. Objects with `ignoresCollision` are treated as non-colliding. Use checkIfObjectsOverlap to ignore that flag.

members.name: checkIfObjectsOverlap
members.summary: Checks collider overlap between two objects without consulting `ignoresCollision`.

signatures.name: checkIfObjectsOverlap
signatures.summary: Checks collider overlap between two objects without consulting `ignoresCollision`.

entries.searchText: collision class physics/collision.ts static collision query helpers. these methods perform read-only overlap checks. they do not apply impulses, move objects, or invoke collision callbacks. for simulation, use the normal `game` physics step or `gameobject.move`. constructor  collision(): collision checkifcolliderscollide collider-based collision check. accepts base collider and dispatches by type tag. callers should ensure colliders are synced first if owner transforms changed. checkifcolliderscollide(c1: collider, c2: collider): boolean checkifobjectscollide collider-based object collision check. objects with `ignorescollision` are treated as non-colliding. use checkifobjectsoverlap to ignore that flag. checkifobjectscollide(obj1: gameobject, obj2: gameobject): boolean checkifobjectsoverlap checks collider overlap between two objects without consulting `ignorescollision`. checkifobjectsoverlap(obj1: gameobject, obj2: gameobject): boolean checkifrectanglescollide generic top-left rectangle overlap check. (still useful for ui / camera / raw bounds checks.) checkifrectanglescollide(x1: number, y1: number, w1: number, h1: number, x2: number, y2: number, w2: number, h2: number): boolean distancesquaredbetweenobjects returns squared distance between two object world positions. distancesquaredbetweenobjects(a: gameobject, b: gameobject): number

entries.name: ColorLightMap
entries.summary: Palette-aware light resolver. A `ColorLightMap` maps a base palette color to corresponding darker and lighter variants at the same index. The renderer queues integer light deltas and resolves them through this class right before presenting the frame.

details.text: `resolve(color, 0)` returns the original color. Negative deltas walk through the darker table; positive deltas walk through the lighter table. If a color is not part of the base palette, darkening falls back to black and lightening falls back to white.

members.name: constructor

details.text: When the arrays are not the same length.

signatures.name: ColorLightMap

details.text: When the arrays are not the same length.

parameters.name: baseColors
parameters.summary: Palette colors that can receive lighting.

parameters.name: darker
parameters.summary: One darker variant for each base color.

parameters.name: lighter
parameters.summary: One lighter variant for each base color.

signatures.text: ColorLightMap(baseColors: readonly RgbColor[], darker: readonly RgbColor[], lighter: readonly RgbColor[]): ColorLightMap

entries.name: Component
entries.summary: Reusable behavior module that can be attached to a GameObject. Components are the main authoring surface for game logic in the engine. A component receives the same lifecycle hooks as its owning object, and can read or mutate the owner through gameObject after it has been attached.

details.text: ```ts class Spin extends Component { update = () => { this.gameObject.setRotation(0, 0, this.gameObject.rotation.z + 2); }; } ```

entries.name: Constraint
entries.summary: Positions an object relative to screen edges or other objects. `Constraint` is useful for UI layout and simple follow-along world layout. Each edge can target `'screen'`, `'object'`, or no target. If both opposing edges are constrained, the object is centered between them after margins.

parameters.name: args
parameters.summary: Constraint arguments. Edge keys are `top`, `left`, `right`, and `bottom`; each accepts `'screen'`, `'object'`, or anything else for none. Object edges use `topObject`, `leftObject`, `rightObject`, and `bottomObject`; margins use the matching `*Margin` keys.

entries.summary: Root runtime controller for a game. `Game` owns the active object list, scene transitions, the fixed update pipeline, collision/rigidbody solving, and draw ordering. Applications create a concrete subclass that implements the abstract lifecycle hooks, then pass it to GameDriver.

details.text: Use instantiate or GameObject.instantiate to add objects. Additions and destroys are queued so object lists remain stable while update callbacks are running.

entries.name: GameDriver

entries.searchText: gamedriver class game/gamedriver.ts browser frame driver for a game. `gamedriver` connects the game loop to `requestanimationframe`, handles fixed timestep catch-up, renders through canvasrenderer, forwards input state to the global input singleton, and coordinates audio pausing when the browser backgrounds the page. constructor  gamedriver(game: game, renderer: canvasrenderer): gamedriver clearhoverposition clears the current hover position. clearhoverposition(): void getrenderproperties returns the renderer dimensions and default color filter. getrenderproperties(): graphicsproperties ispaused returns true when the driver is not currently advancing frames. ispaused(): boolean lifta releases the virtual a action input. lifta(): void liftb releases the virtual b action input. liftb(): void liftc releases the virtual c action input. liftc(): void liftd releases the virtual d action input. liftd(): void liftdown releases the virtual down input. liftdown(): void liftl releases the virtual l shoulder input. liftl(): void liftl2 releases the virtual l2 trigger input. liftl2(): void liftl3 releases the virtual l3 stick-click input. liftl3(): void liftleft releases the virtual left input. liftleft(): void liftr releases the virtual r shoulder input. liftr(): void liftr2 releases the virtual r2 trigger input. liftr2(): void liftr3 releases the virtual r3 stick-click input. liftr3(): void liftright releases the virtual right input. liftright(): void liftup releases the virtual up input. liftup(): void lifty releases the virtual y action input. lifty(): void liftz releases the virtual z action input. liftz(): void pause pauses gameplay and audio until resume is called. pause(): void pointerdown begins a pointer press at screen-space coordinates. pointerdown(x: number, y: number, button?: pointerbutton | null): void pointerdrag moves an active pointer press to new screen-space coordinates. pointerdrag(x: number, y: number, button?: pointerbutton | null): void pointerup releases a pointer press at screen-space coordinates. pointerup(x: number, y: number, button?: pointerbutton | null): void pressa presses the virtual a action input. pressa(): void pressb presses the virtual b action input. pressb(): void pressc presses the virtual c action input. pressc(): void pressd presses the virtual d action input. pressd(): void pressdown presses the virtual down input. pressdown(): void pressl presses the virtual l shoulder input. pressl(): void pressl2 presses the virtual l2 trigger input. pressl2(): void pressl3 presses the virtual l3 stick-click input. pressl3(): void pressleft presses the virtual left input. pressleft(): void pressr presses the virtual r shoulder input. pressr(): void pressr2 presses the virtual r2 trigger input. pressr2(): void pressr3 presses the virtual r3 stick-click input. pressr3(): void pressright presses the virtual right input. pressright(): void pressup presses the virtual up input. pressup(): void pressy presses the virtual y action input. pressy(): void pressz presses the virtual z action input. pressz(): void releaseallinputs releases every virtual key and pointer input. releaseallinputs(): void resume resumes gameplay and audio after a manual pause. resume(): void setarrows sets the current gamepad d-pad/arrows vector. setarrows(x: number, y: number): void sethoverposition updates the current hover position for pointer-aware ui. sethoverposition(x: number, y: number): void setleftstick sets the current gamepad left stick vector. setleftstick(x: number, y: number, directionx: number, directiony: number): void setrenderer swaps the renderer used by the driver. camera viewport dimensions are updated to match the new renderer. entries.name: GameObject
entries.summary: Primary scene entity in the runtime. `GameObject` is the engine's equivalent of a Unity game object: it owns a transform, optional physics/collision state, arbitrary components, lifecycle hooks, and parent/child hierarchy relationships. Game-specific behavior is usually added by attaching Component instances.

details.text: `width` and `height` describe visual/culling bounds. Collision shape and offsets are controlled separately through collider, setCollider, and setColliderOffsets. Use extra for lightweight game-specific state that does not deserve a full component.

members.name: angularDamping
members.summary: Multiplicative angular damping applied after physics resolution.

members.name: angularVelocity
members.summary: Angular velocity in degrees per physics tick.

members.name: canCollideWith
members.summary: Per-object collision filter. Both objects' filters must return `true` for the collision solver to handle the pair.

members.name: collider
members.summary: Optional physics collider owned by this object.

members.name: DEBUG
members.summary: Enables renderer-side debug outlines for this object and its collider.

members.name: DEBUG_COLOR
members.summary: Color used when `DEBUG` outlines are drawn.

members.name: disableCulling
members.summary: When true, this object draws even when outside the camera bounds.

members.name: extra
members.summary: Arbitrary state bag for small gameplay values and prefab arguments.

members.name: friction
members.summary: Surface friction factor used by rigidbody collision impulses.

members.name: fx
members.summary: Accumulated horizontal force, cleared during force integration.

members.name: fy
members.summary: Accumulated vertical force, cleared during force integration.

members.name: hasRigidbody
members.summary: True when a rigidbody component has configured this object for physics.

members.name: height
members.summary: Visual height used for camera culling and debug outlines.

members.name: ignoresCollision
members.summary: When true, this object is ignored by collision checks.

members.name: layer
members.summary: Numeric gameplay layer. The engine reserves WALL_LAYER for map walls.

members.name: linearDampingX
members.summary: Multiplicative damping applied to horizontal velocity.

members.name: linearDampingY
members.summary: Multiplicative damping applied to vertical velocity.

members.name: localPosition
members.summary: Position relative to parent. Root objects mirror world position.

members.name: localRotation
members.summary: Rotation relative to parent.

members.name: localScale
members.summary: Scale relative to parent.

members.name: lockRotation
members.summary: When true, physics ignores angular velocity and torque.

members.name: name
members.summary: Human-readable runtime name used by find helpers.

members.name: position
members.summary: World position in pixels. Mutate through `setPosition` when possible.

members.name: restitution
members.summary: Collision bounce factor from 0 (inelastic) to 1 (fully elastic).

members.name: rotation
members.summary: World rotation in degrees. `z` is the 2D rotation used by renderers/physics.

members.name: scale
members.summary: World scale. Negative values mirror renderers and collider extents.

members.name: torque
members.summary: Accumulated torque, cleared during force integration.

members.name: vx
members.summary: Horizontal velocity in pixels per physics tick.

members.name: vy
members.summary: Vertical velocity in pixels per physics tick.

members.name: width

entries.searchText:
gameobject class game/gameobject.ts primary scene entity in the runtime. `gameobject` is the engine's equivalent of a unity game object: it owns a transform, optional physics/collision state, arbitrary components, lifecycle hooks, and parent/child hierarchy relationships. game-specific behavior is usually added by attaching component instances. constructor  gameobject(name: string): gameobject _childat  _childat(index: number): gameobject _childcount  _childcount(): number _clearcontactflags  _clearcontactflags(): void _clearrigidbody  _clearrigidbody(): void _configurerigidbody  _configurerigidbody(options: { angulardamping: number; friction: number; isstatic: boolean; lockrotation: boolean; mass: number; restitution: number }): void _detachfromhierarchy  _detachfromhierarchy(): void _recordcontactnormal  _recordcontactnormal(nx: number, ny: number): void _refreshinertia  _refreshinertia(): void addcomponent attaches a component to this object. the component's `attach` hook runs immediately. if the object is already active, the engine will call the component's `start` hook during update. addcomponent(component: component): void addforce applies linear force or velocity to a rigidbody object. addforce(x: number, y: number, mode: forcemode): void addtorque applies rotational force or velocity to a non-locked rigidbody. torque is ignored when the object has no rigidbody or `lockrotation` is true. addtorque(torque: number, mode: forcemode): void clearcollider removes the collider and opts this object out of collision checks. clearcollider(): void destroy runs when the lifecycle owner is destroyed and removed from the game. destroy(): void destroy queues an object and its children for destruction. destruction callbacks run at the end of the update phase. destroy(object: gameobject): gameobject draw draws camera-relative world content. draw(r: canvasrenderer): void drawui draws screen-space ui content after world rendering has completed. drawui(r: canvasrenderer): void find finds a descendant by direct name or slash-separated child path. find(name: string): gameobject find finds an active or pending object by name in the current game. find(name: string): gameobject findbyid finds an object by its instance id. findbyid(id: number): gameobject findchild finds a direct child by name. findchild(name: string): gameobject findobjects returns active objects that satisfy `filter`. pending additions are not included in this snapshot. findobjects(filter: (go: gameobject) => boolean): gameobject[] fixedupdate runs before the physics step and regular `update` callback. fixedupdate(): void getchild returns the direct child at `index`. getchild(index: number): gameobject getcomponent returns the first component assignable to `type`. getcomponent<t>(type: (args: any[]) => t): t getcomponents returns attached components. when a component class is provided, only matching instances are returned. without a type, the returned array is the object's live component list. getcomponents<t>(type: (args: any[]) => t): t[]
getcomponents(): component[] instanceid unique integer assigned when this object is constructed. instanceid(): number instantiate queues an object to enter the current game. instantiate(object: gameobject, location?: { x: number; y: number; z: number }): gameobject lateupdate runs after all `update` callbacks, useful for camera following and constraints. lateupdate(): void move kinematically moves this object to an absolute position and resolves static overlaps. this helper is useful for manually controlled objects that should slide out of walls but are not driven by rigidbody forces. it only resolves against static colliders (`invmass === 0`). move(nx: number, ny: number): boolean removecomponent removes a specific component instance. removecomponent(component: component): boolean
removecomponent<t>(type: (args: any[]) => t): t[] setcollider assigns the collider owned by this object. the collider is synced immediately and collision participation is enabled. setcollider(collider: collider): void setcollideroffsets sets collider offset relative to the object transform. offsets are interpreted by the collider type. calling this also enables collision participation. setcollideroffsets(x: number, y: number): void setdimensions sets visual/culling dimensions in pixels. setdimensions(w: number, h: number): void setlocalposition sets local position relative to the current parent. root objects treat local position as world position. setlocalposition(x: number, y: number, z: number): void setlocalrotation sets rotation relative to the current parent, in degrees. setlocalrotation(x: number, y: number, z: number): void setlocalscale sets scale relative to the current parent. setlocalscale(x: number, y: number, z: number): void setposition sets this object's world position. child objects are resynchronized from their local transforms. setposition(x: number, y: number, z: number): void setrotation sets world rotation in degrees. setrotation(x: number, y: number, z: number): void setscale sets world scale. setscale(x: number, y: number, z: number): void setsize sets visual bounds to a square of `n` pixels. setsize(n: number): void start runs once when the lifecycle owner first enters the active game. start(): void translate adds a world-space delta to the current position. translate(x: number, y: number, z: number): void tryfind finds a descendant by direct name or slash-separated child path. tryfind(name: string): gameobject | null tryfind finds an active or pending object by name in the current game. tryfind(name: string): gameobject | null tryfindbyid finds an object by its instance id. tryfindbyid(id: number): gameobject | null tryfindchild finds a direct child by name. tryfindchild(name: string): gameobject | null trygetchild returns the direct child at `index`, or `null` if it does not exist. trygetchild(index: number): gameobject | null trygetcomponent returns the first component assignable to `type`, if present. trygetcomponent<t>(type: (args: any[]) => t): t | undefined update runs once per game tick after physics has been solved. update(): void _hasphysicscontact  property _hasphysicscontact: boolean _hasstablephysicssupport  property _hasstablephysicssupport: boolean _physicssleepframes  property _physicssleepframes: number _physicssleeping  property _physicssleeping: boolean _physicssupporta  property _physicssupporta: gameobject | null _physicssupportb  property _physicssupportb: gameobject | null _physicssupportgraceframes  property _physicssupportgraceframes: number angulardamping multiplicative angular damping applied after physics resolution. property angulardamping: number angularvelocity angular velocity in degrees per physics tick. property angularvelocity: number cancollidewith per-object collision filter. both objects' filters must return `true` for the collision solver to handle the pair. property cancollidewith: (other: gameobject) => boolean collider optional physics collider owned by this object. property collider: collider | null debug enables renderer-side debug outlines for this object and its collider. property debug: boolean debug_color color used when `debug` outlines are drawn. property debug_color: rgbcolor disableculling when true, this object draws even when outside the camera bounds. property disableculling: boolean extra arbitrary state bag for small gameplay values and prefab arguments. property extra: argumentset friction surface friction factor used by rigidbody collision impulses. property friction: number fx accumulated horizontal force, cleared during force integration. property fx: number fy accumulated vertical force, cleared during force integration. property fy: number hasrigidbody true when a rigidbody component has configured this object for physics. property hasrigidbody: boolean height visual height used for camera culling and debug outlines. property height: number ignorescollision when true, this object is ignored by collision checks. property ignorescollision: boolean layer numeric gameplay layer. the engine reserves wall_layer for map walls. property layer: number lineardampingx multiplicative damping applied to horizontal velocity. property lineardampingx: number lineardampingy multiplicative damping applied to vertical velocity. property lineardampingy: number localposition position relative to parent. root objects mirror world position. property localposition: point3 localrotation rotation relative to parent. property localrotation: point3 localscale scale relative to parent. property localscale: point3 lockrotation when true, physics ignores angular velocity and torque. property lockrotation: boolean name human-readable runtime name used by find helpers. property name: string orderinlayer  property orderinlayer: number position world position in pixels. mutate through `setposition` when possible. property position: point3 restitution collision bounce factor from 0 (inelastic) to 1 (fully elastic). property restitution: number rotation world rotation in degrees. `z` is the 2d rotation used by renderers/physics. property rotation: point3 scale world scale. negative values mirror renderers and collider extents. property scale: point3 torque accumulated torque, cleared during force integration. property torque: number vx horizontal velocity in pixels per physics tick. property vx: number vy vertical velocity in pixels per physics tick. property vy: number width visual width used for camera culling and debug outlines. property width: number childcount  accessor childcount invinertia  accessor invinertia invmass  accessor invmass isdestroyed  accessor isdestroyed isgrounded  accessor isgrounded ispendingdestroy  accessor ispendingdestroy istouchingabove  accessor istouchingabove istouchingbelow  accessor istouchingbelow istouchingleft  accessor istouchingleft istouchingright  accessor istouchingright mass  accessor mass parent  accessor parent x  accessor x y  accessor y z  accessor z

entries.name: InputState
entries.summary: Mutable frame-based input state. `InputState` stores the current and previous frame values for virtual direction/action buttons plus a single pointer. Call update exactly once per game tick after gameplay logic has read input so edge helpers such as getKeyDown and isPointerUp advance correctly.

entries.name: Language
entries.summary: Global language bridge for game code. `Language` gives gameplay systems a single way to detect the player's current language across web, editor preview, Android, iOS, and desktop shells. Native hosts can install a LanguageProvider before startup; game code can then call `Language.getLanguage()` without caring where the value came from.

details.text: All values returned from this class are normalized. Localization systems such as generated `.strings` assets use Language.getLanguages to resolve a value in priority order: exact tag, base tag added by the asset manager, asset default language, and finally DEFAULT_LANGUAGE.

members.name: getBaseLanguage
members.summary: Returns only the base language subtag. This is useful when a game has broad translations such as `es` but does not distinguish between regional variants like `es-MX` and `es-ES`.

signatures.name: getBaseLanguage
signatures.summary: Returns only the base language subtag. This is useful when a game has broad translations such as `es` but does not distinguish between regional variants like `es-MX` and `es-ES`.

members.name: getLanguage
members.summary: Returns the current normalized language tag.

signatures.name: getLanguage
signatures.summary: Returns the current normalized language tag.

members.name: getLanguages
members.summary: Returns the normalized preferred-language list. The first entry is always the current language. The list always contains at least one value and falls back to `en` if the provider has no usable data.

signatures.name: getLanguages
signatures.summary: Returns the normalized preferred-language list. The first entry is always the current language. The list always contains at least one value and falls back to `en` if the provider has no usable data.

members.name: onChanged
members.summary: Subscribes to language changes. The listener receives normalized language tags and can safely update menus, subtitles, asset packs, or localization tables. The returned function removes the listener.

signatures.name: onChanged
signatures.summary: Subscribes to language changes. The listener receives normalized language tags and can safely update menus, subtitles, asset packs, or localization tables. The returned function removes the listener.

parameters.name: listener
parameters.summary: Listener called after the normalized language list changes.

members.name: setInstance
members.summary: Installs the active language provider. The provider may be static, backed by browser APIs, or backed by a native bridge. Installing a provider immediately emits a change notification with the normalized current language.

signatures.name: setInstance
signatures.summary: Installs the active language provider. The provider may be static, backed by browser APIs, or backed by a native bridge. Installing a provider immediately emits a change notification with the normalized current language.

parameters.name: p
parameters.summary: Provider used for all future language reads and writes.

members.name: setLanguage
members.summary: Updates the active provider to a single current language. If the provider supports mutation, the call is forwarded. Otherwise the core swaps in an in-memory provider. This makes editor tools and tests easy to drive while still preserving native providers that expose setters.

signatures.name: setLanguage
signatures.summary: Updates the active provider to a single current language. If the provider supports mutation, the call is forwarded. Otherwise the core swaps in an in-memory provider. This makes editor tools and tests easy to drive while still preserving native providers that expose setters.

parameters.name: language
parameters.summary: Raw or normalized language tag.

members.name: setLanguages
members.summary: Updates the active provider with a full preferred-language list. Invalid entries are ignored, duplicates are removed, and English remains the fallback when the list contains no usable values.

signatures.name: setLanguages
signatures.summary: Updates the active provider with a full preferred-language list. Invalid entries are ignored, duplicates are removed, and English remains the fallback when the list contains no usable values.

parameters.name: languages
parameters.summary: Raw or normalized language tags in priority order.

entries.searchText: language class bridge/language.ts global language bridge for game code. `language` gives gameplay systems a single way to detect the player's current language across web, editor preview, android, ios, and desktop shells. native hosts can install a languageprovider before startup; game code can then call `language.getlanguage()` without caring where the value came from. constructor  language(): language getbaselanguage returns only the base language subtag. this is useful when a game has broad translations such as `es` but does not distinguish between regional variants like `es-mx` and `es-es`. getbaselanguage(): string getlanguage returns the current normalized language tag. getlanguage(): string getlanguages returns the normalized preferred-language list. the first entry is always the current language. the list always contains at least one value and falls back to `en` if the provider has no usable data. getlanguages(): readonly string[] onchanged subscribes to language changes. the listener receives normalized language tags and can safely update menus, subtitles, asset packs, or localization tables. the returned function removes the listener. onchanged(listener: languagechangedlistener): () => void setinstance installs the active language provider. the provider may be static, backed by browser apis, or backed by a native bridge. installing a provider immediately emits a change notification with the normalized current language. setinstance(p: languageprovider): void setlanguage updates the active provider to a single current language. if the provider supports mutation, the call is forwarded. otherwise the core swaps in an in-memory provider. this makes editor tools and tests easy to drive while still preserving native providers that expose setters. setlanguage(language: string): void setlanguages updates the active provider with a full preferred-language list. invalid entries are ignored, duplicates are removed, and english remains the fallback when the list contains no usable values. setlanguages(languages: readonly string[]): void instance  accessor instance

entries.name: Lifecycle
entries.summary: Base set of frame callbacks used by scenes, game objects, and components. The engine calls these hooks in a fixed order each frame: `fixedUpdate`, physics, `update`, `lateUpdate`, world `draw`, UI `drawUi`, and finally `destroy` when an object is removed. Subclasses usually override only the hooks they need by assigning arrow functions or methods.

details.text: `draw` is camera-relative world rendering. `drawUi` is screen-space rendering and is not culled by the camera.

members.summary: Runs when the lifecycle owner is destroyed and removed from the game.

entries.name: MapTileCodec
entries.summary: Encoder/decoder for map tile layers. The modern format stores a compact tile table plus base64 fixed-width tile indexes. The codec still understands legacy single-glyph RLE maps so older project files remain loadable.

entries.name: NoopNetworkTransport

entries.name: Note
entries.summary: One encoded synth note event. Durations are measured in engine frames. A frequency of `0` represents a rest. Slide flags tell instruments to interpolate frequency and/or gain into the next note event.

members.name: constructor
members.signature: Note(frequency: number, duration: number, gain: number, slideFrequency: boolean, slideGain: boolean): Note

signatures.name: Note

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.summary: Frequency in hertz, or `0` for a rest.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.summary: Length in engine frames.

parameters.name: gain
parameters.summary: Normalized gain from `0` to `1`.

parameters.name: slideFrequency
parameters.summary: Whether frequency should slide to the next note.

parameters.name: slideGain
parameters.summary: Whether gain should slide to the next note.

entries.name: Pixels
entries.summary: Low-level software pixel buffer. `Pixels` owns the mutable RGB array used by CanvasRenderer. It knows how to draw indexed bitmaps, primitive shapes, strokes, and queued lighting into logical screen coordinates. Most game code should use `CanvasRenderer`; use this class directly only for custom rendering systems.

entries.searchText: rgbcolor class graphics/rgbcolor.ts mutable rgb color value used by the software renderer. a color with any negative channel is treated as transparent/empty by engine drawing code. most apis copy colors before storing them, but low-level pixel buffers mutate instances directly for performance. constructor  rgbcolor(r: number, g: number, b: number): rgbcolor clear sets non-transparent colors to black. no-ops for `none`. clear(): void copy returns a new color with the same channels. copy(): rgbcolor equals returns true when all channels match `other`. equals(other: rgbcolor): boolean gethexcolorstring converts a color into `#rrggbb` css hex format. gethexcolorstring(color: rgbcolor): string grey sets all channels to a grayscale value. no-ops for `none`. grey(v: number): void isnone true when any channel is negative, meaning the color is transparent. isnone(): boolean set copies channel values from another non-transparent color. set(color: rgbcolor): void entries.name: Scene
entries.summary: Base class for a playable scene. A scene owns optional tilemap background data and receives lifecycle hooks just like game objects and components. When a scene starts, it creates static wall objects for tilemap colliders and outer map boundaries before calling the user-defined `start` hook.

entries.name: SpatialAudioSource

entries.name: StaticLanguageProvider
entries.summary: Mutable in-memory language provider. This provider is used as the engine default and is also convenient for tests, editor previews, and browser builds that read from `navigator.languages`.

details.text: `StaticLanguageProvider` stores normalized values only. Subscribers are notified after a real list change and are not notified when the normalized list is identical to the current one.

members.name: getString
members.summary: Resolves a localized string by generated numeric `R.string.*` id.

details.text: This API intentionally accepts an integer resource id instead of an authoring key string. Generated asset managers can then use array-indexed lookup tables and cache the localized values for the active language. Language fallback order is implementation-defined by the asset manager, but generated managers check the current Language.getLanguages list, each base language, the resource default language, DEFAULT_LANGUAGE, and finally the first available value.

signatures.name: getString
signatures.summary: Resolves a localized string by generated numeric `R.string.*` id.

details.text: This API intentionally accepts an integer resource id instead of an authoring key string. Generated asset managers can then use array-indexed lookup tables and cache the localized values for the active language. Language fallback order is implementation-defined by the asset manager, but generated managers check the current Language.getLanguages list, each base language, the resource default language, DEFAULT_LANGUAGE, and finally the first available value.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: Numeric id from the generated `R.string` object.

parameters.name: fallback
parameters.summary: Text returned when `id` is missing or no localized value can be resolved. When omitted, implementations should return `String(id)`.

entries.name: LanguageProvider
entries.summary: Host-platform language provider contract. Browsers, Android, iOS, desktop shells, tests, and editor previews can install one provider through Language.setInstance. Game code should read the static Language API instead of reaching into platform globals. Language tags are expected to be BCP-47-style values such as `en`, `en-US`, `fr-CA`, or `zh-Hant-TW`; the core normalizes invalid or empty values back to DEFAULT_LANGUAGE.

details.text: Providers may be immutable snapshots, mutable in-memory adapters, or live platform bridges. Throwing provider methods are tolerated by the core and resolve to the English fallback, so game code can use Language without defensive platform checks.

members.name: getLanguage
members.summary: Returns the current best language for game content. Providers should return the first language the player prefers. The core will normalize and fall back to `en` if the provider returns an empty, malformed, or throwing value.

signatures.name: getLanguage
signatures.summary: Returns the current best language for game content. Providers should return the first language the player prefers. The core will normalize and fall back to `en` if the provider returns an empty, malformed, or throwing value.

members.name: getLanguages
members.summary: Returns preferred languages in priority order. The first item should match getLanguage. Returning multiple values lets games fall back through increasingly broad localization tables, for example `fr-CA` then `fr` then `en`.

signatures.name: getLanguages
signatures.summary: Returns preferred languages in priority order. The first item should match getLanguage. Returning multiple values lets games fall back through increasingly broad localization tables, for example `fr-CA` then `fr` then `en`.

members.name: onChanged
members.summary: Optional subscription hook for providers that can change while the game runs. Return an unsubscribe function. The core re-broadcasts the update through Language.onChanged after normalizing the language list.

signatures.name: onChanged
signatures.summary: Optional subscription hook for providers that can change while the game runs. Return an unsubscribe function. The core re-broadcasts the update through Language.onChanged after normalizing the language list.

parameters.name: listener
parameters.summary: Provider-level listener. Providers may pass raw or normalized values; the core normalizes again before notifying game listeners.

members.name: setLanguage
members.summary: Optional hook for providers that can be manually updated by a shell or test. Static hosts do not need to implement this. Mutable hosts should normalize or delegate normalization to the core by passing raw values to Language.setLanguage.

signatures.name: setLanguage
signatures.summary: Optional hook for providers that can be manually updated by a shell or test. Static hosts do not need to implement this. Mutable hosts should normalize or delegate normalization to the core by passing raw values to Language.setLanguage.

parameters.name: language
parameters.summary: A raw or normalized language tag. The core passes normalized values when this hook is invoked through Language.setLanguage.

members.name: setLanguages
members.summary: Optional hook for replacing the entire preferred-language list. This is useful when a native platform reports both the current locale and the player's ordered language preferences.

signatures.name: setLanguages
signatures.summary: Optional hook for replacing the entire preferred-language list. This is useful when a native platform reports both the current locale and the player's ordered language preferences.

parameters.name: languages
parameters.summary: Ordered language tags. When invoked through Language.setLanguages, the values have already been normalized and deduplicated.

entries.name: StringResourceDefinition
entries.summary: Generated localized string resource resolved by numeric `R.string.*` ids. `.strings` assets are author-facing source files, but gameplay code should not look up localized text by authoring keys at runtime. During asset generation, each entry is compiled into one numeric id on the generated `R.string` object and one compact `StringResourceDefinition` record.

details.text: Generated asset managers keep these records in an id-indexed table and resolve them through AssetManager.getString. That keeps gameplay call sites fast and stable: ```ts const title = Assets.getString(R.string.menu_title); ```

members.name: defaultLanguage
members.summary: Language used after the player's preferred languages do not contain a value. This comes from the owning `.strings` asset's `defaultLanguage` field. It is normalized by the IDE before generation and should match a key in values when the asset has a default translation.

members.name: values
members.summary: Localized values keyed by normalized language tags. Keys use the same normalized form returned by the core language bridge, such as `en`, `ja`, `pt-BR`, or `zh-Hant-TW`. Values are the final user-facing strings for each language and may intentionally be empty strings.

entries.name: normalizeLanguageTag
entries.summary: Normalizes a language tag for stable game-side comparisons. The normalizer accepts common platform output (`en_US`, `EN-us.UTF-8`, `zh-hant-tw`) and returns a BCP-47-style value (`en-US`, `zh-Hant-TW`). Invalid, blank, `und`, `C`, and `POSIX` inputs return the supplied fallback.

signatures.name: normalizeLanguageTag
signatures.summary: Normalizes a language tag for stable game-side comparisons. The normalizer accepts common platform output (`en_US`, `EN-us.UTF-8`, `zh-hant-tw`) and returns a BCP-47-style value (`en-US`, `zh-Hant-TW`). Invalid, blank, `und`, `C`, and `POSIX` inputs return the supplied fallback.

parameters.name: value
parameters.summary: Raw platform, browser, editor, or test language value.

parameters.name: fallback
parameters.summary: Language returned when `value` cannot be parsed.

entries.searchText: normalizelanguagetag function bridge/language.ts normalizes a language tag for stable game-side comparisons. the normalizer accepts common platform output (`en_us`, `en-us.utf-8`, `zh-hant-tw`) and returns a bcp-47-style value (`en-us`, `zh-hant-tw`). invalid, blank, `und`, `c`, and `posix` inputs return the supplied fallback. normalizelanguagetag(value: unknown, fallback: string): string

members.summary: Axis-aligned or owner-rotated rectangle collider.

entries.name: ForceMode
entries.summary: How a force-like value should be applied to a GameObject.

members.name: Force
members.summary: Accumulate force and apply it during the next physics integration step.

members.name: Impulse
members.summary: Apply an immediate velocity change scaled by inverse mass.

members.name: VelocityChange
members.summary: Apply an immediate velocity change that ignores mass.

entries.searchText: forcemode enumeration physics/forcemode.ts how a force-like value should be applied to a gameobject. force accumulate force and apply it during the next physics integration step. enumeration member force: 0 impulse apply an immediate velocity change scaled by inverse mass. enumeration member impulse: 1 velocitychange apply an immediate velocity change that ignores mass. enumeration member velocitychange: 2

entries.name: InstrumentType
entries.summary: Built-in synthesizer instrument types. Pulse and noise variants map to specific oscillator duty/filter presets used by the retro audio engine.

entries.name: Assets
entries.summary: Global asset manager used by engine helpers and generated game code. Game code usually imports this object directly: ```ts const name = Assets.getString(R.string.player_name); const sprite = Assets.getBitmap(R.drawable.player_idle); ``` Before bootstrap installs a real manager, this is a small dummy provider that throws for most asset types and returns the fallback for localized strings.
entries.searchText: assets variable assets/assets.ts global asset manager used by engine helpers and generated game code. game code usually imports this object directly: ```ts const name = assets.getstring(r.string.player_name); const sprite = assets.getbitmap(r.drawable.player_idle); ``` before bootstrap installs a real manager, this is a small dummy provider that throws for most asset types and returns the fallback for localized strings.

entries.name: Audio
entries.summary: Global audio facade. Use this for synthesized retro clips, decoded sound effects, and background music without constructing an audio player manually.
entries.searchText: audio variable audio/audio.ts global audio facade. use this for synthesized retro clips, decoded sound effects, and background music without constructing an audio player manually.

entries.searchText: core_component_keys variable game/component/corecomponents.ts stable asset keys for engine-provided components. the ide stores these keys in component assets and maps them back to runtime constructors through instantiatecorecomponent.

entries.name: DEFAULT_LANGUAGE
entries.summary: Default engine language used whenever a platform does not provide a usable locale. The value is deliberately the short English language subtag (`en`) instead of a regional variant such as `en-US`, because gameplay code can safely use it as a broad fallback key for translated strings.
entries.searchText: default_language variable bridge/language.ts default engine language used whenever a platform does not provide a usable locale. the value is deliberately the short english language subtag (`en`) instead of a regional variant such as `en-us`, because gameplay code can safely use it as a broad fallback key for translated strings.
